propositions: The non-int PID, missing-colon-separator, and owner-file read-error branches of _mkdir_lock_is_stale are untested.
A table-driven test should assert _mkdir_lock_is_stale returns False for each malformed or unreadable owner-stamp form.
An unreadable owner file is deterministically simulated by placing a directory at the owner-file path so open raises IsADirectoryError.
A mid-write partial stamp (hostname only, colon not yet written) exercises the missing-separator branch and must return False.
The safety property requires that malformed or unreadable stamps never trigger lock reclamation.
Cases must cover empty file, missing colon, empty host, empty/non-numeric/negative/zero PID, extra delimiters, and foreign host.
